GIT INTERNALS Cover & Contents Contents Cheatsheet

A Digital Adventure / Code Quest

GIT
INTERNALS

Boot up the terminal and go spelunking inside .git — blobs, trees, commits, branches, and the SHA-1 fingerprints that hold it all together. Retro terminal, modern explanations, zero hand-waving.

Narrated by The Clear Mentor · example-first, gotchas included · a patient senior engineer at your side

Contents

  1. Introduction: Why Git Internals Matter start here
  2. The .git Folder: Git's Secret Hideout the base camp
  3. Git Objects: Blobs, Trees, and Commits blue, green & amber
  4. SHA-1 Hashes: Git's Fingerprint System every object's ID
  5. Branches and HEAD: Pointers, Not Copies the compass
  6. Commits Are Snapshots, Not Diffs the camera
  7. Merging and Rebasing: Combining Histories joining paths
  8. The Staging Area (Index): Git's Waiting Room before the snapshot
  9. Reflog: Git's Time Travel Log the safety net
  10. Reset and Revert: Undoing Mistakes handle with care
  11. Stashing: Temporary Storage for In-Progress Work the side pocket
  12. Tags and Releases: Marking Important Moments planting a flag
  13. Remote Repositories: How Push and Pull Work talking to origin
  14. Git Hooks: Automating with Scripts tripwires
  15. Performance and Optimization: Git Under Load packing it in
  16. Security and Integrity: Trusting Your History tamper-proofing
  17. The Future of Git: What's Next? the horizon
  18. The Cheatsheet every command, one spread
How To Read

Use → and ← to turn pages, ↑ to jump back here. Each chapter opens with a quick refresher, so it's fine to jump straight to the one you're curious about. Watch for Pro Tip and Warning boxes — the warnings mark commands (like reset --hard) that can genuinely lose work.

Contents Begin ›
⌂ Library
